mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0%, tight labor markets accelerate wage growth and consumer spending, driving corporate earnings upward and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth combined with rising retail sales indicates synchronized demand-supply expansion, which boosts corporate profit margins and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising INDPRO and RSAFS fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, rising policy rates compress equity multiples, but when housing starts exceed 1.2 million units, durable demand buffers corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns turn negative in > 60% of triggered instances despite HOUST > 1.2M.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP indicates expanding aggregate demand, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 growth rate remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns show no statistical difference from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index rises above 2.5% annualized, persistent inflation erodes real purchasing power and forces aggressive policy tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances where CPI annualized growth crosses above 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid employment growth paired with declining unemployment creates a wage-price spiral that squeezes corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict negative 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, industrial production rebounds faster than consumer demand, causing inventory drawdowns and price markups that boost corporate profitability and yield positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth > GDPC1 growth in 3 months
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of instances where INDPRO outpaces GDPC1 growth.
